html:
Exclusive to:

The Sea Sack is a Backpack introduced in the Shipwrecked DLC, used to expand the character's inventory. It reduces the rate of spoilage of perishable items contained within by 50% (making such items last twice as long). It requires 5 Seaweeds, 2 Vines, and 1 Shark Gills to craft and an Alchemy Engine to prototype. The Sea Sack functions identically to the Insulated Pack from the Reign of Giants DLC except that it can float on the ocean.

As with all backpacks, the Sea Sack cannot be placed in inventory or Chests but can be dropped on the ground and used for storage. Like the Piggyback and Krampus Sack, it is not flammable. Holding an item over a Sea Sack that is on the ground will give the 'Store' option, allowing the Sack to be filled without equipping it.

Despite slowing the spoilage of Food items by the same degree, the Sea Sack does not work exactly like an Ice Box. It will not stop the spoilage of Ice and Ice Cubes and will not cool a Thermal Stone.

Tips

  • The Sea Sack could be used as a temporary storage for food in case of the players Ice Boxes malfunctioning due to flooding during the Monsoon Season.
    • Alternatively, it can be used as a "sea Ice Box" if the player intends to fish with Trawl Nets multiple times in a row, and the fishing spot is far away from their base.

Trivia

  • Sea Sacks were introduced in the Release the Quacken update.

Gallery

  • Wilson wearing a Sea Sack.
  • Willow wearing a Sea Sack.
  • Wolfgang wearing a Sea Sack.
  • Wendy wearing a Sea Sack.
  • WX-78 wearing a Sea Sack.
  • Wickerbottom wearing a Sea Sack.
  • Woodie wearing a Sea Sack.
  • Wes wearing a Sea Sack.
  • Maxwell wearing a Sea Sack.
  • Wigfrid wearing a Sea Sack.
  • Webber wearing a Sea Sack.
  • Walani wearing a Sea Sack.
  • Warly wearing a Sea Sack.
  • Wilbur wearing a Sea Sack.
  • Woodlegs wearing a Sea Sack.
  • Wilba wearing a Sea Sack.
  • Wormwood wearing a Sea Sack.
  • Wheeler wearing a Sea Sack.

Exclusive to:
Sea Sack
"Keeps your food fresher, longer!"
×5 ×2 ×1
Tab
Tier
Does not stack
Perk
+8 Inventory slots.
Slows stored food spoilage.
"seasack"
“I hate when food has that not-so-fresh taste.”
–Wilson
“I have to wear this soggy thing on my back?”
–Willow
“Wolfgang can carry even more things!”
–Wolfgang
“It slows the rot and decay.”
–Wendy
“LOCKS IN FRESHNESS”
–WX-78
“Keeps my books and provisions cool!”
–Wickerbottom
“It's for holding my treasures.”
–Woodie
“It won't make me stronger, but it'll give me more pockets.”
–Maxwell
“A sack that protects perishables, but it is made from perishables itself.”
–Wagstaff
“Disgusting, but useful in its way.”
–Wigfrid
“Eww, it's wet and squishy.”
–Webber
“Squishy.”
–Walani
“Wetter is better.”
–Warly
“Me travels moist'ly hereout.”
–Woodlegs
“'TIS A BURDEN SACK”
–Wilba
“Keeps belly stuff fresh”
–Wormwood
“Refrigerated pocket space!”
–Wheeler
The Sea Sack is a Backpack introduced in the Shipwrecked DLC, used to expand the character's inventory. It reduces the rate of spoilage of perishable items contained within by 50% (making such items last twice as long). It requires 5 Seaweeds, 2 Vines, and 1 Shark Gills to craft and an Alchemy Engine to prototype. Holding an item over a Sea Sack that is on the ground will give the 'Store' option, allowing the Sack to be filled without equipping it.
Despite slowing the spoilage of Food items by the same degree, the Sea Sack does not work exactly like an Ice Box. It will not stop the spoilage of Ice and Ice Cubes and will not cool a Thermal Stone.
Tips[edit | edit source]
The Sea Sack could be used as a temporary storage for food in case of the players Ice Boxes malfunctioning due to flooding during the Monsoon Season.
Alternatively, it can be used as a "sea Ice Box" if the player intends to fish with Trawl Nets multiple times in a row, and the fishing spot is far away from their base.
Trivia[edit | edit source]
Sea Sacks were introduced in the Release the Quacken update.

summaryViHtml:
Tóm tắt

Bao Biển là một Ba Lô được giới thiệu trong DLC ​​Shipwrecked, dùng để mở rộng kho đồ của nhân vật. Nó làm giảm tỷ lệ hư hỏng của các mặt hàng dễ hư hỏng chứa trong đó xuống 50% (làm cho các mặt hàng đó tồn tại lâu gấp đôi). Nó cần 5 Rong biển, 2 Dây leo và 1 Mang Cá mập để chế tạo và Máy Luyện kim để tạo nguyên mẫu. Bao Biển có chức năng giống hệt với Gói Cách Nhiệt trong DLC ​​Reign of Giants ngoại trừ việc nó có thể nổi trên biển.
